subject: Project Management
title: The Name 'Waterfall'
description: Learn The Name 'Waterfall' in one minute.

hook.text: Royce never used the word 'waterfall' in his paper. The name came later — and it stuck perfectly.

buildup.text: The cascade diagram showed phases flowing down like water: each step pours into the next with no way back up.

discovery.text: The term first appeared in a 1976 paper by Bell and Thayer, describing Royce's model for the Department of Defense.

twist.text: The name perfectly captures the model's fatal flaw: water doesn't flow uphill. Neither does waterfall development.

climax.text: The name 'waterfall' became both a description and a criticism — a method where going back is impossibly expensive.

punchline.text: They named it waterfall because you can't push water back up.

quiz.question: Who coined the term 'waterfall' for the development model?
options: Bell and Thayer in 1976, not Royce himself
